title: Fortune, Fate, and the Natural History of the Sarlacc

{{Quote|Now, now, Grubbat. You knew what the penalty would be if you were caught double-dipping in the podracing books.|[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]|translated=1|<ref name="FFNHS">{{Tales|6|Fortune, Fate, and the Natural History of the Sarlacc}}</ref>}}
"'''Fortune, Fate, and the Natural History of the Sarlacc'''" is a 9-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] featured in [[Star Wars Tales 6|''Star Wars Tales'' #6]]. It was written by [[Mark Schultz]], the artwork was done by [[Kellie Strom]] and it was edited by [[Philip Amara]].

==Plot summary==
[[File:BabySarlacc-SWT06-FFNHS.jpg|thumb|left|The [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] and his new catch]]
[[Grubbat Fhilch]] gambled with his life and lost. Above a [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] pit, he curses [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], while a [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] Guard pushes Grubbat into the pit. Grubbat vows to have his revenge, though Fett finds this to be an unlikely prospect.

Later, the Sarlacc releases its offspring to the wind. The Sarlacc "baby" attaches itself to a [[Dewback/Legends|dewback]] ridden by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Desert stormtrooper/Legends|sandtroopers]], and sucks blood from the animal to feed itself.

Later on, the troopers order a group of [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] to clean their dewbacks. A Jawa removes the "baby" from one of the dewbacks and puts it in a bottle of water. Unknowing of what it may be, the Jawa uses it as a cleaning sponge on a droid. At the Jawa camp, the "baby" manages to escape the bottle when the Jawa is called into a nearby tent for his supper, and clasps itself to a birdlike creature, feeding itself on its catch.

As the sarlacc "baby" grows, it challenges a larger, spider-like creature with three heads. It seems to lose, with the other creature eating the sarlacc larva. However, as the creature eventually lies down to sleep for the night, it dies and is eaten from the inside by the sarlacc. After finishing its meal, the sarlacc digs itself into a pit.

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Jabba the Hutt and Boba Fett take [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]], to be thrown into the very sarlacc whose life cycle has been told in the story. The story ends with the assertion that Grubbat will soon have his unexpected revenge.

==Continuity==
The story implies that Grubbat gets his revenge for Boba Fett when the sarlacc eats him. However, the story ''[[A Barve Like That: The Tale of Boba Fett]]'' reveals that Fett later escaped from the belly of sarlacc.
